The Rapid Sustainment Modernization (RSM) program objective is to rapidly modernize sustainment practices, enhancing readiness and cutting costs by identifying, assessing, developing, validating, verifying, and deploying cutting-edge technologies across the sustainment enterprise. This program achieves this by proactively engaging stakeholders across the spectrum, from warfighters to depot and field maintenance teams, supply chains, and program offices, to pinpoint critical enterprise needs. In direct support of the Secretary of War direction to increase readiness across the Department of War, efforts within this PEC will be prioritized based on the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA), USAF Force Design, Agile Combat Employment (ACE), and the Defense Planning Guide (DPG). These efforts will support work and collaboration efforts across the Combat Readiness Directorate in PEs 78051F, 71212F, 72833F, 64706F, 65827F, 22834F, 52834F, 28031F, 41135F and 27521F to increase readiness across the Air Force enterprise. The program maintains flexibility to adapt to the rapidly evolving technology landscape, pivoting technical areas and solutions as requirements, warfighter needs, and resource environments change. Advanced Manufacturing Program Office (AMPO) leverages innovative technologies to create superior products, refine existing ones, and cultivate advanced production capabilities. This strengthens manufacturing quality and processes, providing warfighters with a distinct competitive advantage. The Advanced Manufacturing (AdvM) program develops additive and other advanced manufacturing capabilities to produce tools, jigs, fixtures, and aircraft components, thereby mitigating diminishing manufacturing sources, addressing material shortages, and accelerating part procurement lead times. Repair Additive Manufacturing (RAM) technologies enhance maintenance capabilities, minimize downtime, and boost operational efficiency. Furthermore, the program develops deployable AdvM and RAM capabilities for contested environments, enabling on-demand parts and tools at the point of need, along with on-site and aircraft-side repair capabilities. Automation and Robotics (A&R) efforts focus on hazardous environments, material handling, and repair, assembly, and test systems. Robotic Laser Paint Removal systems exemplify this, eliminating the need for human presence in hazardous and confined spaces. Expeditionary Combat Technologies (ECT) develops and delivers deployable structures, power and water generation tools, logistics tools to reduce logistics footprints, and enhance Airmen mission effectiveness in expeditionary and austere environments. Prototyping across these areas facilitates the integration and demonstration of emerging technologies, accelerating their transition into warfighter capabilities through iterative development and increased utilization of commercial technology. Digital Architectures and Capabilities (DAC) is comprised of Conditioned Based Maintenance Plus (CBM+), Digital Materiel Management (DMM), and Maintenance Digital Ecosystem (MxDE). Conditioned Based Maintenance Plus is a collaborative Department of War readiness initiative centered on data analysis to transition unscheduled to scheduled maintenance, driving mission readiness improvements. It employs a failure management/data-driven decisions strategy to perform predictive/proactive maintenance, leveraging Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML) capabilities to identify evidence of need to perform maintenance. Sensor based algorithms leverage AI/ML to transform onboard sensor data into component degradation detection alerts, enabling decision advantage. Training Developments program is part of Pilot Training Transformation (PTT) program initiative to modernize pilot training, improve both quality and quantity of pilots through implementing advanced technology and up-to-date instructional techniques. In 2018, as part of this initiative, Air Education and Training Command (AETC) launched the Pilot Training Next (PTN) program in an effort to produce more pilots in less time at a reduced cost. PTN explores technological and instructional innovations designed to improve upon Specialized Undergraduate Pilot Training (SUPT) at Located at Randolph Air Force Base, TX, Detachment 24. PTN program train small groups of students using experimental syllabi, and in 2018, the first class of 13 students graduated from a 24-week course. PTN graduates receive a basic aeronautical rating and go to follow-on training in airframes like the F-35/F-16 fighter aircraft and C-17 cargo aircraft. In 2022, innovations tested at Detachment 24 were adopted and AETC launched a new pilot training syllabus at the all major pilot training bases. Training Developments program harvests cutting edge technology to provide a faster, more cost effective and comprehensive training model to get the warfighter in the cockpit in half the time. Training Developments program will utilize new training technologies to develop effective processes and procedures to increase pilot production, improve and streamline existing training programs that will directly aid AETC's flying training enterprise to address the Air Force's pilot shortage. The Contracting Information Technology (CON-IT) system enables the Department of the Air Force (DAF) to accomplish its mission effectively and securely in today's rapidly changing and increasingly contested cyber domain. The Air and Space Forces require a single, 21st century contract management solution to enable DAF mission execution, from acquiring and sustaining weapon system platforms, to supporting contingency operations overseas. CON-IT supplies this single solution by consolidating and replacing numerous aging and increasingly unsupportable, legacy contract writing and management systems, while enabling the Air and Space Forces to procure vital capability faster and with increased data accuracy through built-in automation. Specifically, CON-IT's functionality provides contract data sharing interoperability across all DAF contracting communities and external business partners such as Defense Contract Management Agency, Defense Finance and Accounting Service, and industry partners. In addition, CON-IT facilitates the execution of the DAF's $240+ billion annual budget, ensuring global procurement operations are timely, auditable, and secure. CON-IT enables the DAF to anticipate and respond to the changing pace and dynamic nature of processes, regulations, compliance and technologies across the contracting domain. It empowers the contracting community to comply with Financial Improvement Audit Readiness (FIAR). This requirement supports performance of a full financial audit as required by title 10 U.S.C. Chapter 9A, Sec 240-D. CON-IT was the Department of War's (DoW) first contract writing system meeting Section 862 of FY13 NDAA requirements implementing DoW Procurement Data Standards (PDS). In addition, CON-IT implements Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 (as amended) to make Electronic and Information Technology (EIT) accessible to people with disabilities. CON-IT directly supports the aim of improving government efficiency and fiscal responsibility. Specifically, it addresses objectives focused on Federal Procurement, Defense Acquisitions, Innovation, and Waste Reduction. By streamlining processes and enhancing compliance, CON-IT will provide unprecedented visibility into acquisition, supply chain, and budget execution, enabling data-driven, cost-effective decision-making. The Combat Survivor Evader Locator (CSEL) System provides aircrews with end-to-end global satellite secure emergency communication capability during combat and peace-time flying operations. CSEL provides a hand held radio as part of the mandatory aircrew survival gear. CSEL is a joint program (Air Force, Army, Navy, and US Marine Corps) and is the DoD program of record for personnel recovery survival radios. CSEL supports four of five Personnel Mission Phases: Report, Locate, Support, and Recover. A National Security Agency (NSA) Cryptographic Modernization mandate and the Ultra High Frequency Follow-On satellite constellation are at the end of life and are driving upgrades to base stations. This effort includes development to modernize the system to integrate common waveforms, integrate broadcast reception for non- CSEL devices. A portable interrogator will precisely locate the IP using the CSEL architecture without extensive audio communications that could compromise both rescue forces and IP. The portable interrogator will enable communication between IP and any designated aircraft not equipped with a AN/ARS-6 PLS Lightweight Airborne Radio System (LARS) or similar device as a Rescue Support Force (F-35, F-16, F-15E, Ground Teams etc.) asset. programElements.mission: The U.S. Space Command Research and Development program will ensure a comprehensive military advantage in space by integrating existing space capabilities from the Armed Services and the Intelligence Community with innovations from national laboratories and commercial partners. The program will promote responsible behavior in space, advocate greater space capabilities, and collaborate with industry partners to achieve national space objectives. This will be accomplished through the acceleration of technology demonstrations, rapid operational prototyping, and experimentation to increase lethality. The RDT&E program will also conduct assessments of current and possible future space-based effects via model-based analysis. The Capability and Verification Environment (CAVE) Lab will support the prioritized analysis of space systems, including: • Position, Navigation and Timing (PNT) • Satellite Communications (SATCOM) • Missile Warning/Golden Dome • Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (ISR) capabilities and Integration • Space Domain Awareness (SDA) and sensing needs • Orbital Warfare systems • Ground Infrastructure/Command and Control (C2) studies This will inform service capability roadmaps and funding to meet the required effects for the Space Area of Responsibility (AOR). This effort is in Budget Activity 4, Advanced Component Development and Prototypes (ACD&P), because efforts are necessary to evaluate integrated technologies, representative modes or prototype systems in a high fidelity and realistic operating environment.
